assertions.title: Typed input executes on the remote shell and resulting output is rendered in order

steps.action: Open the real SSH fixture session through MainActivity and send `printf 'VISIBLE_INPUT_UI_PROOF\n'` through the visible terminal input field and send button
steps.expected: The typed command is transmitted to the remote shell through the user-visible input path
steps.observed: `real_fixture_terminal_ui_proves_visible_input_scrollback_live_bottom_copy_and_multiline_paste` used `session_input_field` plus `session_send_button` and reached the live fixture shell on `10.0.2.2:3122`.

steps.action: Wait for the terminal transcript to render the command and response
steps.expected: The transcript shows the echoed command and then the remote output in order
steps.observed: The rerun waited for `VISIBLE_INPUT_UI_PROOF` and verified the echoed `printf 'VISIBLE_INPUT_UI_PROOF\n'` command appeared before the output line in the transcript.

steps.action: Confirm the session remains live afterward
steps.expected: The prompt returns after the remote response
steps.observed: The test stayed in the connected fixture session after the ordered command/response proof completed.

evidence.network: Real SSH fixture session from emulator to 10.0.2.2:3122 remained connected while the visible input control dispatched the command and returned remote output.

assertions.title: Long or multiline output remains readable and scrollback preserves earlier transcript

steps.action: Send a long real-fixture command that prints 48 numbered lines plus multiline output
steps.expected: The terminal renders long and multiline output continuously
steps.observed: The rerun sent a long command through the visible session input and waited for `SCROLL_LINE_48` and `MULTILINE_GAMMA` from the live SSH fixture transcript.

steps.action: Scroll upward into older transcript content
steps.expected: Earlier lines remain readable after the viewport fills
steps.observed: The test scrolled terminal history until `SCROLL_LINE_01` was visible and the terminal status changed to `viewing history`.

steps.action: Inspect transcript continuity across the long-output surface
steps.expected: Earlier transcript is preserved rather than truncated or broken
steps.observed: The copied terminal transcript and history state still contained early lines, multiline markers, and later output from the same live fixture run.

evidence.network: Long and multiline output came from the real SSH fixture session at 10.0.2.2:3122, not a scripted transcript substitute.

assertions.title: The user can return to the live bottom after inspecting older scrollback

steps.action: Enter history mode after producing long real-fixture output
steps.expected: The user can inspect older transcript content without ending the live session
steps.observed: The rerun scrolled to older output and asserted `session_terminal_status` showed `viewing history`.

steps.action: Allow new output to arrive while still viewing history
steps.expected: The session keeps running even while the user is away from the live bottom
steps.observed: The test remained in history mode while delayed live output was still pending from the same fixture command.

steps.action: Use the jump-to-live affordance
steps.expected: The user returns to the live bottom and sees newly arriving output
steps.observed: Clicking `session_jump_to_live` cleared the `viewing history` state and the rerun then observed `LIVE_BOTTOM_RESUME_PROOF` in the live transcript.

evidence.network: The delayed `LIVE_BOTTOM_RESUME_PROOF` line arrived from the active SSH fixture session after returning to the live bottom.

assertions.title: Copy and paste work through the terminal surface, including multiline paste

steps.action: Copy visible terminal output from the live session
steps.expected: The terminal copy action places transcript text on the clipboard
steps.observed: Clicking `session_copy_button` surfaced `Copied terminal output` and the clipboard contained `SCROLL_LINE_01`, `MULTILINE_ALPHA`, and `LIVE_BOTTOM_RESUME_PROOF`.

steps.action: Prepare multiline clipboard content and paste it back through the terminal surface
steps.expected: The paste path delivers multiline content without dropping order or characters
steps.observed: The rerun loaded three `printf` lines into the clipboard, clicked `session_paste_button`, and saw `Pasted from clipboard` on the session UI.

steps.action: Inspect remote execution order after paste
steps.expected: The remote shell receives the pasted multiline content in order
steps.observed: The live fixture transcript rendered `PASTE_VISIBLE_LINE_1`, `PASTE_VISIBLE_LINE_2`, and `PASTE_VISIBLE_LINE_3` in order.

evidence.network: Clipboard paste was executed against the live SSH fixture session on 10.0.2.2:3122 and the resulting remote output preserved multiline order.

assertions.title: Opening and closing the soft keyboard keeps the terminal readable, focused, and interactive

steps.action: Open the real fixture session and focus the visible terminal input field to show the software keyboard
steps.expected: The IME becomes visible from the real session surface without breaking the live session
steps.observed: `fixture_orchestration_handles_ime_rotation_background_and_relaunch_with_real_app_shell` called `showImeForTaggedField("session_input_field")` and asserted the IME was visible while the session stayed connected to `10.0.2.2:3122`.

steps.action: Close the IME through lifecycle-mediated session transitions and return to the app shell
steps.expected: The terminal remains readable and the prompt/input area stays reachable after keyboard closure
steps.observed: After rotation, background/resume, and activity recreation, the test reopened the real session screen, still showed `Connected to 10.0.2.2:3122.`, retained transcript proof, and kept `session_input_field` and `session_send_button` reachable.

steps.action: Confirm the terminal remains interactive after keyboard visibility changes
steps.expected: Continuing work remains coherent after the keyboard opens and closes
steps.observed: The same live session continued producing remote output (`ORCHESTRATION_BACKGROUND_RESUME_OK`) after the keyboard-visible phase, and visible input/send controls remained present for continued interaction.

evidence.network: The live SSH fixture session stayed truthful across IME visibility changes and subsequent lifecycle transitions on emulator-5554.

assertions.title: Orientation changes preserve truthful session and transcript state

steps.action: Rotate the device during an active real fixture session
steps.expected: The session stays usable or returns with a truthful state after rotation
steps.observed: The orchestration rerun rotated to landscape during an active live session and reopened the real app shell on the shared emulator.

steps.action: Inspect the post-rotation session surface
steps.expected: Connected/disconnected state remains truthful and transcript context remains available
steps.observed: After rotation the UI still showed `Connected to 10.0.2.2:3122.`, retained the remote proof transcript, and remained on one live session.

steps.action: Check that the rotated terminal is still a real live PTY session
steps.expected: Rotation does not leave a fake-live shell behind
steps.observed: The rerun captured different remote PTY sizes before and after rotation, confirming the rotated surface was still bound to the real remote session.

evidence.network: Real SSH fixture session to 10.0.2.2:3122 remained connected and truthful across device rotation, with remote PTY size changes observed after rotation.

assertions.title: Backgrounding and resuming the app preserves or truthfully reports session state

steps.action: Background the app from an active real fixture session and resume it
steps.expected: The resumed UI preserves or truthfully reports the actual live session state
steps.observed: The orchestration rerun used `backgroundAndResumeApp(appPackage = APP_PACKAGE)` during a live fixture session and reopened the session screen through the real app shell.

steps.action: Inspect the resumed session surface
steps.expected: The user is not shown stale transcript content as if it were live
steps.observed: After resume the UI still showed `Connected to 10.0.2.2:3122.` and preserved the existing remote proof transcript from the same live session.

steps.action: Continue work after resume
steps.expected: The resumed session remains genuinely usable if still connected
steps.observed: The rerun sent `ORCHESTRATION_BACKGROUND_RESUME_OK` to the remote shell and observed that output line after resume, confirming truthful continued interactivity.

evidence.network: Background/resume preserved a live SSH fixture session to 10.0.2.2:3122 and allowed further remote output after resume.

assertions.title: Unexpected remote-side or network disconnects are surfaced quickly and stop live interaction

steps.action: Connect to the real fixture session and trigger the fixture-side disconnect path from the visible terminal UI
steps.expected: A real unexpected disconnect is induced against the live SSH session
steps.observed: `unexpected_fixture_disconnect_trigger_truthfully_requires_reconnect_through_visible_terminal_ui` connected to `10.0.2.2:3122`, proved the live remote session, and then sent the fixture disconnect trigger through the terminal UI.

steps.action: Wait for the session state to change after the remote shell closes
steps.expected: The terminal moves quickly to a truthful disconnected/reconnect-needed state
steps.observed: The rerun observed `SessionConnectionState.RECONNECT_REQUIRED`, `reconnectRequired == true`, and status `Remote shell closed for 10.0.2.2:3122.` within the test timeout.

steps.action: Inspect post-disconnect interactivity
steps.expected: Live input stops and the UI no longer looks actively connected
steps.observed: The UI showed reconnect-required/truth-banner messaging, removed the disconnect button, relabeled the host action to `Reconnect`, and disabled input, send, and paste controls.

evidence.network: The live SSH fixture session on 10.0.2.2:3122 was intentionally dropped from the remote side and the terminal surface transitioned to reconnect-required with live input disabled.

assertions.title: Process death or activity recreation restores only a truthful session state

steps.action: Recreate the activity around an active real fixture session
steps.expected: After recovery the reopened app shows only a truthful session state rather than a fake-live shell
steps.observed: The orchestration rerun called `recreateActivityAndWaitForAppShell(appPackage = APP_PACKAGE)` during a live session and reopened the session screen through the real app shell.

steps.action: Inspect the restored session after activity recreation
steps.expected: The UI matches the real connection state and keeps prior transcript context appropriately
steps.observed: After recreation the session still showed `Connected to 10.0.2.2:3122.` and retained prior remote proof text from the same live session.

steps.action: Check that the restored session remains a usable real session rather than stale UI chrome
steps.expected: Visible input controls match the truthful restored state
steps.observed: The rerun verified `session_input_field` and `session_send_button` were present after recreation while the same real session stayed connected.

evidence.network: Activity recreation restored the real SSH fixture session state truthfully on emulator-5554 without presenting a stale dead session as live.

frictions.description: The current instrumentation harness emits XML/HTML/log artifacts but does not automatically generate screenshot files for these UI reruns.

The focused rerun covered nine contract assertions through three instrumentation methods, so each assertion had to be mapped back to concrete method-level evidence from the test source and generated reports.
frictions.resolution: Mapped each assigned assertion to the exact rerun method(s) in SessionSshFixtureInstrumentedTest and confirmed all three methods passed in the copied XML/HTML evidence.

summary: Retested 9 assigned terminal/lifecycle assertions on emulator-5554 with focused real-fixture instrumentation. PASS: VAL-TERM-001, VAL-TERM-002, VAL-TERM-003, VAL-TERM-004, VAL-TERM-006, VAL-TERM-007, VAL-TERM-008, VAL-TERM-010, VAL-TERM-011. No assigned assertions remain blocked in this rerun.
